📨 Creating/updating PRs... plnd/O8762-add-frontmatter-life-03-05-0831: https://app.graphite.com/github/pr/dagster-io/erk/8766 (updated) Checked out PR #8766 at /Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-01 ➜ erk-slot-01 git:(plnd/O8762-add-frontmatter-life-03-05-0831) claude
▐▛███▜▌ Claude Code v2.1.69 ▝▜█████▛▘ Haiku 4.5 · Claude Max ▘▘ ▝▝ ~/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-01
❯ if i run @.claude/commands/erk/objective-plan.md without an argument will it infer the objective number ⎿ Read .claude/commands/erk/objective-plan.md (238 lines)
⏺ Yes. From the command's Step 1 logic:
1. It checks the current branch name 2. Looks for .erk/impl-context/<branch>/ref.json to find the associated plan ID 3. Runs erk exec get-plan-metadata <plan-number> objective_issue to get the objective 4. If found, uses it automatically and tells you: "Using objective # from current branch's plan #. Run with explicit argument to override."
If no objective is found via that path, it falls back to prompting you with AskUserQuestion.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ❯ ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ➜ (git:erk) (wt:erk
i think we should also infer from current branch if has OXXXX as prefix and infer the XXXX is the objective

1→--- 2→title: Branch Name Inference 3→last_audited: "2026-02-08 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→tripwires: 6→ - action: "changing branch naming convention (plnd/ prefix)" 7→ warning: "The plnd/ prefix (planned-PR) is a cross-cutting contract used by branch creation, extraction functions, and PR recovery. Changing the prefix format requires updating all consumers. The legacy P{issue}- prefix has been fully removed." 8→ - action: "adding branch_name to plan-header at creation time" 9→ warning: "branch_name is intentionally omitted at creation because the branch doesn't exist yet. The plan-save → branch-create → impl-signal lifecycle requires this gap. See the temporal gap section below." 10→ - action: "recovering a branch name from a PR number using UI or truncated display" 11→ warning: "Use `gh pr view <pr-number> --json headRefName` to recover exact branch names. UI display may truncate long branch names." 12→read_when: 13→ - "debugging missing branch_name in plan issues" 14→ - "implementing PR lookup from plan issues" 15→ - "modifying branch creation or naming conventions" 16→--- 17→ 18→# Branch Name Inference 19→ 20→## The Temporal Gap Problem 21→ 22→Plan metadata (`plan-header` block) intentionally omits `branch_name` at creation time because of a temporal gap in the plan lifecycle: 23→ 24→1. **Plan saved** → GitHub issue created (no branch exists yet) 25→2. **Branch created** → User runs `erk br co --for-plan {issue}` (branch now exists) 26→3. **Implementation starts** → `impl-signal started` writes `branch_name` into the plan-header 27→ 28→The gap between steps 1 and 2 is unavoidable: the plan must exist before the branch can be named after it (the branch name contains the issue number). Attempting to set `branch_name` during save would require either predicting the branch name (fragile — the user might choose a custom name) or creating the branch immediately (wrong — the user hasn't started work yet). 29→ 30→## Two-Layer Resolution
... [53 lines omitted] ...
40→If Layer 1 does not produce a match, the command fails with an actionable error. 65→Common failure scenarios and their fixes:
session.assistant:

Let me look at the exact function to confirm the pattern.

PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Plan: Add branch-name objective inference to objective-plan command Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master) (plan:#8751)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:master"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create new branch and planned PR. Stays on current branch." - Create a new branch, save plan as draft PR. 2. "Implement without saving" - Implement directly on the current branch without saving a plan PR. 3. "View/Edit the Plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
⚠️ WARNING: Currently on 'master'. We strongly discourage implementing directly on the trunk branch. Consider saving the plan and implementing in a dedicated worktree instead.
If user chooses 'Create new branch and planned PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. Call ExitPlanMode to end the planning session.
If user chooses 'Implement without saving': 1. Create implement-now marker: erk exec marker create --session-id 0161ba92-0e8c-46d5-9a4a-a89301208afe \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly on the current branch. Do NOT run 'erk exec setup-impl' or create a new branch.

1→# Plan: Add branch-name objective inference to objective-plan command 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→When running `/erk:objective-plan` without arguments on a branch like `plnd/O8762-add-frontmatter-life-03-05-0831`, the command currently goes through a multi-step plan metadata lookup to find the objective number. Adding a direct branch-name extraction step would be faster and work even when plan metadata isn't available. 6→ 7→## Change 8→ 9→Edit `.claude/commands/erk/objective-plan.md` Step 1 to add a new inference path **before** the plan metadata lookup: 10→ 11→**Current order:** 12→1. Look up plan metadata via `ref.json` → `get-plan-metadata` (existing fallback) 20→4. Fall back to prompting 21→ 22→### Specific edit to `.claude/commands/erk/objective-plan.md` 23→ 24→After the "Get current branch name" step (line 55), add a new sub-step before the `ref.json` check: 25→ 26→``` 27→2. Check if the branch name contains an objective number directly: 28→ - Pattern: `^pl(?:an(?:ned)?|nd)/[Oo](\d+)-` (e.g., `plnd/O8762-some-slug-01-15-1430`) 29→ - If matched, use the extracted number as the objective and inform the user: 30→ "Using objective #<number> from branch name. session.assistant:
Here's the plan:
# Plan: Add branch-name objective inference to objective-plan command
## Context
When running `/erk:objective-plan` without arguments on a branch like `plnd/O8762-add-frontmatter-life-03-05-0831`, the command currently goes through a multi-step plan metadata lookup to find the objective number. But the objective number (`8762`) is right there in the branch name. Adding a direct branch-name extraction step would be faster and work even when plan metadata isn't available.
## Change
Edit `.claude/commands/erk/objective-plan.md` Step 1 to add a new inference path **before** the plan metadata lookup:
**Current order:** 1. Check for explicit argument 2. Look up plan metadata via `ref.json` → `get-plan-metadata` 3. Fall back to prompting
**New order:** 1. Check for explicit argument 2. **Extract objective from branch name** using regex `^pl(?:an(?:ned)?|nd)/[Oo](\d+)-` 3. Look up plan metadata via `ref.json` → `get-plan-metadata` (existing fallback) 4. Fall back to prompting
## Files to modify
- `.claude/commands/erk/objective-plan.md` — lines ~50-93 (Step 1 inference logic)
---

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': '📋 Plan: Add branch-name objective inference to objective-plan command\nCurr...[truncated 463 chars]...ion': 'Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

session.tool_result:
User has answered your questions: "📋 Plan: Add branch-name objective inference to objective-plan command Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master) (plan:#8751)
What would you like to do with this plan?"="Create new branch and planned PR". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) /erk:plan-save --objective=123 # Explicit objective link (overrides marker) /erk:plan-save --current-branch # Use current branch instead of creating new plnd/ branch ```
Objective linking: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command. Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan).
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. The JSON output contract includes `plan_number`, `plan_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`.
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type`, `--objective`, and `--current-branch` flags:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--objective=<number>": - Extract the number - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to "--objective=<number>" Else: - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--current-branch": - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to "--current-branch" Else: - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 2: Generate Branch Slug
**Skip this step if `--current-branch` was passed.** The current branch is used directly.
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 3: Generate Plan Summary
Write a concise 2-3 sentence summary of the plan. This summary will be visible at the top of the PR description (above the collapsed full plan).
Guidelines:
- Focus on WHAT the plan does and WHY - Do not repeat the title - Plain text, no markdown headers or formatting - Avoid special shell characters (backticks, dollar signs) - Store as PLAN_SUMMARY
### Step 4: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, summary, and optional flags.
If CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG is set, pass `--current-branch` (no branch slug needed):
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="0161ba92-0e8c-46d5-9a4a-a89301208afe" --current-branch --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Otherwise, pass the branch slug:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="0161ba92-0e8c-46d5-9a4a-a89301208afe" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `plan_number` for verification in Step 5.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 5: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 4.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <plan_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close draft PR #<plan_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 6: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "0161ba92-0e8c-46d5-9a4a-a89301208afe" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --pr "#<plan_number>" --status in_progress ```
This atomically fetches the objective body, finds the matching node row, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → plan #<plan_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 7: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as #<plan_number> (duplicate skipped)`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options.
**Otherwise, on success**, display:
``` Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<plan_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options (implement in current worktree, implement in new worktree, or done).
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
### Step 5: Push Planning Session
After saving successfully, push the planning session for cross-machine learning. This captures the in-progress session state so it's available even if implementation happens on a different machine.
Run `erk exec capture-session-info` to get the session file path, then push it:
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id "0161ba92-0e8c-46d5-9a4a-a89301208afe" 2>/dev/null || true ```
If the plan was saved successfully and `plan_number` is known, use push-session directly for better stage tracking:
```bash erk exec push-session \ --session-file "<session_file_path>" \ --session-id "0161ba92-0e8c-46d5-9a4a-a89301208afe" \ --stage planning \ --source local \ --plan-id <plan_number> \ 2>/dev/null || true ```
Where `<session_file_path>` is obtained from `erk exec capture-session-info` output and `<plan_number>` is from Step 2's JSON output.
**Note:** This is non-critical. If it fails, the plan was still saved successfully. The `2>/dev/null || true` ensures graceful degradation.
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the plan number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved plan number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "0161ba92-0e8c-46d5-9a4a-a89301208afe" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="0161ba92-0e8c-46d5-9a4a-a89301208afe" --bra...[truncated 164 chars]...ching the plnd/O{number} pattern, before falling back to the slower plan metadata lookup."
Save plan as draft PR via erk exec plan-save

tool_use.param: erk exec push-session --session-file "/Users/schrockn/.claude/projects/-Users-schrockn-cod...[truncated 58 chars]...46d5-9a4a-a89301208afe" --stage planning --source local --plan-id 8776 2>/dev/null || true
Push planning session for cross-machine learning

tool_use.param:
# Plan: Add branch-name objective inference to objective-plan command
## Context
When ru...[truncated 1954 chars]... regex matches the same pattern as `extract_objective_number()` in `erk_shared/naming.py`

session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: Plan #8776 saved successfully.
Display ALL of the following next-steps commands to the user as plain text. Show every line exactly as written — do NOT summarize, truncate, or omit any lines. (do NOT use AskUserQuestion — just display the text):
